ops.title: settings.json "reverts" were stale local checkouts, not fragment gaps — root cause + heal recipe
ops.body:
## What happened (2026-07-13)

A `.claude/settings.json` diff appeared to *remove* `"disableArtifact": true` and per-marketplace `"autoUpdate": true`, suggesting stale cc-guides fragments. Investigation across the full fleet found the fragments were never the problem:

1. `plugin/guides/json/settings-base.json` gained `disableArtifact` (cc-skills `4d193f8`, 07-12 05:41) and `autoUpdate` on all 4 marketplace entries (`ab68f3e`, 07-12 21:41).
2. An **artifact-only hand-stamp sweep at 07-12 22:50** added `autoUpdate` to the captain-hook entry in ~21 *local working copies* — the self-reverting class (edit fragments, never artifacts).
3. The daily guides crons re-rendered **every remote main green by 07-13**.
4. Local checkouts still held pre-key rendered content + the stamp, so any diff/rebase against healed main read as *removing* both keys — the "revert". Committing `@` wholesale in such a checkout would push a real regression.

## Heal recipe (stale shared checkout, jj-colocated)

`jj git fetch` → rebase `@` onto `main@origin` if behind → `jj restore --from main@origin .claude/settings.json .claude/fragments/cc-guides.lock .claude/fragments/.claude` (only these paths — sibling fragment dirs can hold concurrent sessions' WIP). Plain-git repos (docker-dsl, room-scan): `git fetch` + `git checkout origin/main -- <paths>` + `git merge --ff-only origin/main`.

## Gaps closed this pass

- Guard test `plugins/repo-bootstrap/tests/test_guides_fragments.py` now asserts every settings-base marketplace entry sets `autoUpdate` (cc-skills `1762ed05`).
- captain-hook's custom local-only settings fragment gained `disableArtifact` (captain-hook `24691954`).
- cc-orchestrate's overlay marketplace entry gained `autoUpdate` (cc-orchestrate `bc8b2f8e`); its long-red vuln CI fixed via go 1.26.5 directive bump (`5ace4299`).

## Known exceptions (correct as-is)

- **yasyf.com**: settings.json exists but is NOT fragment-managed (only AGENTS/CLAUDE fragments); missing both keys until its settings-from-fragments migration lands.
- **captain-hook / cc-sentiment local checkouts**: parked on concurrent feature stacks whose base predates the keys; the stacks never touch settings.json, so rebasing onto main inherits the healed file — do not "heal" someone else's checkout position.

## Sharp edge

`ccx vcs ship` in a shared checkout parked on a foreign bookmark advances and pushes **that bookmark** (captain-hook incident: swept commits onto `yasyf/lag-probe-ch`; rewound via `jj bookmark set --allow-backwards` + push, then `jj rebase -r <commit> -d main`). In shared working copies prefer explicit `jj commit <fileset>` + `jj bookmark move main --to @-` + `jj git push --bookmark main`.
